message:
chore(beads): add durability program archive diagnostics and verification issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Acceptance: common ephemeral/test entrypoints use isolated storage automatically or fail loudly, and focused tests prove tmp-style runs do not contaminate the operator mailbox archive.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:02.585025852Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:02.585025852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","recovery","storage","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:02.585025852Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Acceptance: repair/reconstruct captures the original db, sidecars, integrity/probe output, and environment summary into a forensic bundle before any destructive quarantine or rebuild step.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:03.742521002Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:03.742521002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","db","doctor","forensics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:03.742521002Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Acceptance: targeted coverage exists for reconcile, isolation, scan, normalize, forensics, and messaging; residual risks are documented explicitly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:04.883016701Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:04.883016701Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","test","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.6","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:17:04.883016701Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.3","title":"DB/storage audit: guard and reservation enforcement edge cases","description":"Audit the pre-commit guard and reservation matching logic for fnmatch symmetry, rename/move handling, archive reads, stale ownership, and false positive/false negative enforcement cases.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:09:14.927180826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T00:03:33.531228482Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T00:03:33.530454273Z","close_reason":"Completed: guard slice audited; generated hook fail-open bug fixed and targeted remote verification passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","guard","reservations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:09:14.927180826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1264,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Guard audit finding on 2026-03-19: the generated Python Git hook was failing open on Git command errors in the live pre-commit and pre-push paths. `get_staged_files()` swallowed CalledProcessError/Exception and returned an empty list, and `get_push_files()` silently continued on rev-list/diff-tree failure. That could disable reservation enforcement during exactly the kinds of repo/tooling failures where fail-closed behavior is needed. Fixed by emitting explicit stderr diagnostics and exiting 2 for staged/push inspection failures. Added executable regression tests that render the hook script and verify both pre-commit and pre-push fail closed when git is unavailable.","created_at":"2026-03-19T00:02:52Z"}]}

Focus on state-machine drift, missing visibility, cooldown/throttle bugs, wrong anchors, incorrect output contracts, and stale tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:07:25.875458882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:10:44.349002762Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","audit","http","robot","server"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.6.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:07:25.875458882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-repqw","title":"T5.3: Auto-handshake messaging and blocking checks order parity","description":"Auto-handshake note appended when auto_contact_if_blocked was tried: 'Automatic handshake attempts already ran for: {agents}; wait for approval or retry the suggested calls explicitly.' Blocking checks order: (1) allow self, (2) check block_all, (3) check auto+recent, (4) check AgentLink, (5) add to blocked. Order must match.","notes":"Implemented blocking-check order parity in send_message contact enforcement by introducing contact_policy_decision() and applying deterministic recipient iteration (to/cc/bcc). Decision order now explicitly: self -> block_all -> auto+recent -> approved link -> blocked. auto+recent combines thread/reservation auto-allow and recent contact set. Also kept auto-handshake attempted note behavior and CONTACT_REQUIRED payload/message parity from prior step in this same surface. Added tests: contact_policy_decision_self_allowed_even_if_block_all, contact_policy_decision_block_all_overrides_recent_and_approved, contact_policy_decision_auto_recent_then_link, contact_policy_decision_contacts_only_requires_link, contact_policy_decision_invalid_policy_defaults_to_auto. Validation via rch: cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools contact_policy_decision -- --nocapture (pass), cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools contact_ -- --nocapture (pass), cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools (pass).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverFox","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:04:31.609148519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:21:44.934288623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:21:44.934269597Z","close_reason":"Completed auto-handshake note + blocking checks order parity with unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"]}
 {"id":"br-rjr4z","title":"T2.4: Unit tests for markdown rendering and syntax highlighting","description":"Comprehensive tests for the markdown rendering pipeline and syntax highlighting integration.

TEST CASES:

Markdown rendering:
- Heading levels H1-H6 produce correct styles (bold + different colors)
- Bold, italic, strikethrough, inline code render with correct Modifiers
- Ordered and unordered lists render with correct indentation and bullets
- Nested lists (3 levels deep) render with increasing indentation
- Tables render with proper column alignment (left, center, right)
- Blockquotes render with styled left border
- Links render with accent color and underline
- Task lists render with checkbox characters
- Empty markdown produces empty output
- Malformed markdown degrades gracefully (no panic)

Syntax highlighting:
- JSON code block produces correct token coloring (keys, strings, numbers, booleans)
- Rust code block highlights keywords, types, strings
- Unknown language falls back to monospace plain text
- Auto-detection identifies JSON from leading '{' or '['
- Empty code block produces empty highlighted output
- Very long code blocks (1000+ lines) complete within 50ms

Sanitization:
- HTML script tags stripped by ammonia
- HTML img tags with javascript: URLs stripped
- Nested HTML exploitation attempts neutralized
- Clean markdown passes through unchanged

LOGGING:
Each test logs the input markdown, the rendered span count, and any sanitization actions taken.

Target: 20+ tests.

FILES: tui_widgets.rs or tui_screens/messages.rs test module","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 10+ markdown rendering tests covering all GFM element types
- [ ] 5+ syntax highlighting tests covering JSON, Rust, fallback
- [ ] 3+ sanitization tests for malicious content
- [ ] Performance test: 1000-line code block highlights in < 50ms
- [ ] All tests log diagnostic details with tracing

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeRobin","created_at":"2026-02-13T19:59:54.596028994Z","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:19:10.721348174Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:19:10.721326303Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["markdown","rendering","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rjr4z","depends_on_id":"br-127ka","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rjr4z","depends_on_id":"br-2jn7d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rjr4z","depends_on_id":"br-36n1c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1164,"issue_id":"br-rjr4z","author":"OrangeRobin","text":"Completion evidence (OrangeRobin):

Added/verified test coverage in markdown rendering surface:
- code_fence_priority_languages_render_content (json/python/rust/javascript/bash)
- code_fence_unknown_language_falls_back_without_losing_content
- sanitize_body_strips_script_and_style_tags
- sanitize_body_blocks_javascript_urls
- sanitize_body_preserves_markdown_syntax
- looks_like_json_rejects_fenced_json_code_block
- render_detail_with_json_body_no_panic
- long_code_block_render_timing_diagnostic (1000-line fence with elapsed_ms diagnostic output)

Validation attempts were executed via RCH but full server test execution is currently blocked by unrelated in-progress compile breakage in threads.rs (duplicate tree_item_to_widget_node / API drift in br-78etn workstream).","created_at":"2026-02-15T03:19:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-rk4gw","title":"[track] T1: Advanced Data Visualization Engine — LineChart, BarChart, Canvas","description":"Replace basic sparklines and hand-rolled gauges with frankentui's native chart widgets for
dramatically richer data visualization across Dashboard, ToolMetrics, SystemHealth, and Analytics.

CURRENT STATE:
- Dashboard uses Sparkline (basic ASCII sparkline) for throughput
- ToolMetrics shows per-tool data as text tables with inline bars
- SystemHealth has MetricTile sparklines and ReservationGauge (custom widget)
- Analytics has basic aggregation displays

AVAILABLE BUT UNUSED FRANKENTUI CHART WIDGETS:
- ftui_extras::charts::BarChart — Horizontal/vertical bars with labels, colors, grouping
- ftui_extras::charts::LineChart — Multi-series line charts with legends, fill, gridlines
- ftui_extras::canvas::Canvas — Sub-pixel drawing via Braille/HalfBlock/Block modes
  - Canvas::Mode::Braille gives 2x4 resolution per cell (160x200 in 80x50 terminal)
  - Canvas::Mode::HalfBlock gives 1x2 resolution per cell
  - Canvas::Mode::Block gives 1x1 (standard character resolution)
- Painter trait for custom Canvas drawing (lines, circles, rectangles, text)

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Dashboard throughput panel: LineChart with 60s window, gradient fill, legend
2. Dashboard activity heatmap: Canvas::Braille with time-on-X, event-type-on-Y
3. ToolMetrics latency distribution: BarChart showing P50/P95/P99 per tool
4. ToolMetrics call frequency: LineChart with multi-series (one per tool)
5. SystemHealth memory/CPU: LineChart with dual Y-axis
6. SystemHealth connection topology: Canvas with node-link diagram
7. Analytics trend panels: LineChart with configurable time windows
8. Animated chart updates: Smooth data point transitions (not jarring redraws)

DATA PIPELINE:
- Charts need time-series data. The EventRingBuffer (10K events, thread-safe) provides raw events.
- Create ChartDataProvider trait that aggregates EventRingBuffer data into chart-ready time-series.
- Aggregate at configurable intervals (1s, 5s, 30s, 1m, 5m) for different zoom levels.
- Cache aggregated data to avoid re-computing every frame.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- tui_screens/dashboard.rs — Replace sparkline with LineChart, add Canvas heatmap
- tui_screens/tool_metrics.rs — Add BarChart and LineChart panels
- tui_screens/system_health.rs — Add LineChart for resources, Canvas for topology
- tui_screens/analytics.rs — Add trend LineCharts
- tui_widgets.rs — Add ChartDataProvider trait and aggregation logic
- tui_theme.rs — Add chart_series_1..6 palette colors if not present

RISK: Canvas rendering performance. Braille mode renders 8 dots per cell, which is CPU-intensive
for large areas. Mitigate by capping canvas size and using dirty-rect optimization.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] LineChart renders on Dashboard with 60s throughput window
- [ ] BarChart renders on ToolMetrics with P50/P95/P99 bars
- [ ] Canvas Braille heatmap renders on Dashboard
- [ ] All charts use theme palette colors (chart_series_1..6)
- [ ] Animated transitions when new data points arrive
- [ ] Frame render stays < 16ms with charts active
- [ ] Charts degrade gracefully on tiny terminals (< 80 cols)

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:30:33.793984208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:30:33.793907014Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charts","frankentui","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rk4gw","depends_on_id":"br-1xt0m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1165,"issue_id":"br-rk4gw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION GUIDE (2026-02-13, RubyPrairie):

FRANKENTUI CHART API RESEARCH NOTES:

The chart widgets live in ftui-extras which is already a workspace dependency (Cargo.toml line 47):
  ftui-extras = { path = '../frankentui/crates/ftui-extras', features = ['theme', 'syntax', 'export', 'markdown', 'charts', 'text-effects'] }

The 'charts' feature gate is ALREADY ENABLED. Key types:
- ftui_extras::charts::BarChart — Group-able bar charts with configurable orientation
- ftui_extras::charts::LineChart — Multi-series with Dataset, Axis, LegendPosition
- ftui_extras::canvas::Canvas — Sub-pixel drawing (feature: canvas in ftui-extras)

VERIFY: Check if 'canvas' feature is in the current feature list. If not, add it.
Look at: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/Cargo.toml for available feature gates.

DATA PIPELINE DESIGN RATIONALE:

The EventRingBuffer (tui_events.rs) stores 10K events with thread-safe access. It has:
- events_since_seq(seq) -> Vec<MailEvent> for incremental reads
- try_iter_recent(n) -> last N events
- filter_by_kind(kind) -> filtered events
- replay_range(start, end) -> range replay

The ChartDataProvider trait MUST NOT re-scan the full ring buffer every frame.
Instead: maintain a cursor (last_processed_seq) and incrementally aggregate only new events.
This is critical for the 16ms frame budget.

AGGREGATION APPROACH:
- Use ring buffer of fixed-size buckets (e.g., 60 buckets of 1 second each)
- On new events: increment the current bucket
- On time advance: rotate buckets (oldest dropped)
- This gives O(1) per-frame aggregation cost regardless of event volume

CANVAS PERFORMANCE NOTES:
Braille mode renders 2x4 dots per character cell. For an 80x10 area, that's 160x40 = 6,400 dots.
Each dot requires a color lookup and bit manipulation. At 60fps this is ~384K operations/sec.
This is well within budget, but avoid using Braille for areas larger than ~100x20 cells.
For larger areas, use HalfBlock mode (1x2 per cell, 50% the work).","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:09:25Z"},{"id":1166,"issue_id":"br-rk4gw","author":"LilacLake","text":"Progress update: started and implemented transition plumbing via child  (status now in_progress). Added reusable chart interpolation helper + dashboard/tool-metrics wiring; validation currently blocked by unrelated  compile failures in workspace checks.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:06:01Z"},{"id":1167,"issue_id":"br-rk4gw","author":"LilacLake","text":"Correction to prior comment text (shell quoting issue).

Progress update: started and implemented transition plumbing via child br-3jz52 (status now in_progress).
- Added reusable chart interpolation helper and tests in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_widgets.rs.
- Wired animated throughput rendering in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/dashboard.rs.
- Wired animated latency bar rendering in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/tool_metrics.rs.
- Validation currently blocked by unrelated workspace compile failures in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/cache.rs.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:06:08Z"},{"id":1168,"issue_id":"br-rk4gw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified both children (br-1k84y T1.1, br-1f7td T1.6) are closed. Track complete.","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:46:53Z"}]}
+{"id":"br-rqv3i","title":"Durability program: improve startup and doctor messaging for root-cause clarity","description":"## Summary
Clarify startup and doctor outputs so operators immediately understand the blocking root cause instead of getting buried in secondary warnings.

## Scope
- make startup failure summaries lead with the actual stop condition
- keep massive reconstruct/archive warning sets concise in terminal output while preserving full artifacts
- ensure archive hygiene issues are surfaced separately from live corruption incidents
- add coverage for the operator-facing text paths","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:19:56.538388695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:24:13.141315022Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","ops"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rqv3i","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:19:56.538388695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-s4vqd","title":"Fix flaky disk tests, value_as_i64 float truncation, and query_delta NaN","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"NobleGlen","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:25:43.525170852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:26:01.544776950Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:26:01.544757784Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bugfix","correctness"],"comments":[{"id":1169,"issue_id":"br-s4vqd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Three correctness fixes:

1. **disk.rs flaky tests** (core crate): read_proc_io_bytes_returns_non_zero_on_linux and sample_and_record_updates_io_bytes_metrics assumed /proc/self/io read_bytes > 0. On warm caches or in containers, physical disk read_bytes is legitimately 0. Fixed to test consistency (non-decreasing) rather than non-zero.

2. **value_as_i64 float truncation** (db crate): Float/Double values outside i64 range silently truncated via `as i64` cast. Added range guards so out-of-range values return None. Added 5 targeted tests.

3. **query_delta NaN** (server crate): f64 subtraction in query timing could produce NaN if metrics are corrupted, since NaN.max(0.0) returns NaN. Added is_finite guard.

Validation: 902 core tests pass, 1990 DB tests pass, clippy 0 warnings on both crates.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:25:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-shfc","title":"T2.6: Tests for flake-triage CLI (artifact parsing, multi-seed, reproduction)","description":"## Objective
Create strong automated coverage for `am flake-triage` behavior across parsing, reproduction, and multi-seed detection.

## Work
- Add unit/integration tests for artifact scanning edge cases and classification logic.
- Verify reproduction command generation and failure-path handling.
- Validate deterministic output/exit semantics expected by automation consumers.

## Deliverable
A robust test suite that protects flake-triage correctness during ongoing evolution.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:37.532567227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:15:40.742512138Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:15:40.742492692Z","close_reason":"Added 17 integration tests (15 active, 2 ignored) for flake-triage CLI in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/tests/flake_triage_integration.rs. Tests cover: flag parsing for scan/reproduce/detect, artifact scanning with fixtures, malformed artifact handling, JSON schema validation, timestamp sorting, and help output. Also fixed 4 pre-existing compile errors in robot.rs (function signature mismatches for build_agents, build_thread, and CliError variant).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-shfc","depends_on_id":"br-32ax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1170,"issue_id":"br-shfc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T2.6: Tests for Flake-Triage CLI

## What to test

### Unit tests
1. **Artifact parsing**: Create sample *.failure.json files in a temp dir, verify
   scan_artifacts() finds and parses them correctly
2. **Malformed artifact handling**: Verify scan_artifacts() skips invalid JSON files
   with a warning (not a panic)
3. **Grouping**: Verify artifacts are grouped by test_name correctly
4. **Flake rate computation**: Given known pass/fail counts, verify flake_rate and
   is_flaky are correct
5. **Edge cases**: 0 seeds, 1 seed, all pass, all fail, empty directory

### Integration tests
6. **CLI flag parsing**: Verify all three subcommands accept their respective flags
7. **Scan with test fixtures**: Create a temp dir with fixture artifacts, run
   `am flake-triage scan --dir <tmp> --json`, verify JSON output structure
8. **Detect with a known-passing test**: Run detect against a test that always passes,
   verify is_flaky=false
9. **JSON output schema**: Verify all JSON outputs have consistent field naming

### Considerations
- Reproduce and detect tests require cargo test to be available. Mark these as
  #[ignore] for CI environments without a Rust toolchain, or use a mock Command.
- For scan tests, create fixture artifacts inline (no external file dependencies).

## Location

### Overview
The `am share wizard` command delegates to a Python script (`share_to_github_pages.py`) that implements the interactive deployment wizard. The Rust code in `lib.rs` handles script resolution, Python execution, and exit code passthrough.

### Script Resolution Flow
The wizard script is resolved in priority order:
1. `{cwd}/scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` (in-bundle script)
2. `{source_root}/scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` (repo scripts dir)
3. `{source_root}/legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` (legacy fallback, only when running from source tree)

### Key Functions (lib.rs lines 4316-4433)
- `resolve_share_wizard_script(cwd)` → ShareWizardScriptResolution
- `run_share_wizard_in_cwd(cwd)` → orchestrates resolution and execution
- `run_python_script_in_cwd(script, cwd, pythonpath)` → Python subprocess
- `is_readable_file(path)` → existence + read check

### User-Visible Outputs
| Scenario | Output | Stream |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Wizard start | `Launching deployment wizard...` | stdout |
| Script not found | `Wizard script not found.` | stderr |
| Script not found | `Expected locations: {paths}` | stderr |
| Script not found | `This command only works when running from source.` | stderr |
| Script not found | `Run the wizard directly: python scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` | stderr |
| Python missing | Falls back to `python3` command | - |
| Success/Failure | Python script output passthrough | stdout/stderr |

### Exit Codes
| Code | Condition |
|------|-----------|
| 0 | Python script exits successfully |
| 1 | Script not found |
| 1 | Python/python3 command not found |
| N | Passthrough from Python script exit code |

### Edge Conditions
1. **Missing Python**: If `python` fails with ENOENT, retries with `python3`
2. **Missing script**: Fails with exit code 1 and detailed error message
3. **Non-source-tree invocation**: Legacy fallback only available when cwd is within source tree
4. **Legacy PYTHONPATH**: When using legacy script, adds `legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/src` to PYTHONPATH
5. **Exit code passthrough**: Python exit code passed through unchanged (uses `.code().unwrap_or(1)`)

### PYTHONPATH Handling
When using the legacy script path, the PYTHONPATH is augmented:
- Prepends `{repo_root}/legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/src`
- Preserves existing PYTHONPATH entries

### Existing Test Coverage (lib.rs)
1. `share_wizard_prints_guidance_when_script_not_found` - verifies error messages
2. `share_wizard_resolves_when_running_from_repo_root` - verifies resolution
3. `share_wizard_runs_stub_script_and_passthrough_exit_code` - verifies exit code passthrough

### Decisions for Native Implementation
| Behavior | Keep/Change | Rationale |
|----------|-------------|-----------|
| Python delegation | **CHANGE** | Native Rust implementation removes Python dependency |
| Exit code mapping | **KEEP** | Existing consumers expect these codes |
| Error messages | **KEEP** | Maintain UX consistency |
| Script resolution | **CHANGE** | No longer needed once native |
| PYTHONPATH handling | **REMOVE** | Not applicable to native |

### Validation Strategy
1. **Unit tests**: Test resolution logic, error message formatting
2. **Integration tests**: Test with mock bundle directories
3. **E2E tests**: End-to-end wizard flow with actual deployment targets

### Native Rust Implementation Notes
The native implementation should:
- Implement the same interactive prompts as the Python wizard
- Support GitHub Pages and Cloudflare Pages targets
- Emit the same user-visible progress messages
- Preserve exit code semantics
- Add structured JSON output mode (`--json`)

### Logging/Artifact Expectations
- Wizard progress should be logged to stderr
- Final result should be logged with structured info
- For `--json` mode, emit JSON to stdout with:
  - `success: bool`
  - `target: string` (github-pages, cloudflare-pages)
  - `url: string` (deployed URL)
  - `bundle_path: string`
  - `error: string` (if failed)
